# Beings Marketing Site — Codebase Routing Map

> This file primes AI agents with the architecture of the Beings marketing site.
> Section headers are stable anchors — `context-packer.ts` and the `/prime` command
> grep for them by name. Keep `##` headings unique within this file.

## Orientation

- `AGENTS.md` — Agent identity (Senior Frontend Engineer), tech stack, triad framing
- `docs/architecture.md` — Narrative architecture (Canvas + Card, layouts, islands,
  positioning contract, sovereignty model, Lines We Don't Cross)
- `docs/bw-prd-01-rebrand.xml` — Product Requirements Document (SSOT for component
  inventory at P0/P1/P2, integration spec, implementation phases)
- `beings-local/docs/vision.md` — **Positioning SSOT.** Authoritative source for
  what Beings is, who it serves, and which promises the site may make. Consult
  before any copy change that introduces or modifies a product claim.
- `.cursor/rules/website-project-context.mdc` — Project context rule (always-on)
- `.cursor/rules/website-architecture.mdc` — Directory layout reference
- `.cursor/rules/website-astro.mdc` — Astro component conventions
- `.cursor/rules/website-react-islands.mdc` — React island patterns
- `.cursor/rules/website-tailwind.mdc` — Tailwind + brand token usage
- `.cursor/rules/brand.mdc` — Brand design guide + anti-patterns
- `planning/INDEX.md` — Active specs (when created); spec files: `planning/<NNNN>-*.xml`

## Project Overview

Beings is sovereign agent infrastructure that does **digital chores** and
**Intelligent Document Processing (IDP)** for SMB and mid-market teams —
**locally deployed to your environment** ("Air-Gapped Sovereignty"), with
**Zero-Retention Enterprise Cloud** orchestration for complex reasoning, and
progressive migration of cloud workloads to local as customer hardware
upgrades. This workspace is the **public marketing site** at
`https://website.beings.com` (Cloudflare Pages project name: `beings-marketing`,
alias `beings-marketing.pages.dev`).

The site is a **static site** generated by Astro 5 (`output: 'static'`) with
React 19 used only as *interactive islands*. The site is built and deployed via
`npm run build` → `dist/` → `npx wrangler pages deploy dist`.

Workspace classification: **GREEN** — public GitHub, no PII processing.

The marketing site uses the same brand tokens, the same "Canvas + Card" visual
language, and (per page) the same `AppShell` layout as the product itself.
That visual fidelity is in service of a single message: Beings gives knowledge
workers their time and attention back by doing digital chores privately,
securely, and under their control — never on a SaaS data-extraction model.

## Positioning Source of Truth

Two artefacts govern what this site is allowed to say:

- **`beings-local/docs/vision.md`** — **positioning SSOT.** What Beings is,
  who it serves, which promises the site may make. All copy that introduces
  or modifies a product claim is checked against this file first.
- **`docs/bw-prd-01-rebrand.xml`** — **implementation SSOT.** Which pages
  ship, which components exist, with which priorities and integrations.

When the two disagree, the vision doc wins on positioning and the PRD is
updated to match.

## Buyer Personas

The site is written in parallel for two audiences:

- **Operators** — SMB and mid-market team leads, ops managers, founders,
  and individual knowledge workers drowning in inbox triage, CRM hygiene,
  spreadsheet reconciliation, and document handling.
- **Approvers** — **CISOs, Data Protection Officers, and Heads of Digital
  Transformation** who must sign off before any tool touches internal data.
  Every page an operator might forward to their security team must survive
  that read.

The site is **not** written for solo consumers, academic researchers,
journalists, or interview-transcription users. That positioning is dead.

## Deployment Model

Beings is sold as **Productized Deployment** (Palantir-style), not as
self-serve SaaS. The commercial offer is a three-step ladder:

1. **Workshop** — half-day educational + qualifying engagement (~£1,200
   working hypothesis).
2. **Pilot / 6-Week Deployment Bootcamp** — time-boxed engagement on a
   specific chore, deliverable includes an AI Strategy artefact (~£5k
   baseline working hypothesis, bespoke for larger scopes).
3. **Annual License** — productized deployment under multi-year contract
   (~£24k/year working hypothesis).

Pricing is exploratory and not canonical until validated post-workshop
with Beth Gladstone (w/c 25 May 2026). The site CTAs are **"Book a Pilot"
/ "Book a Discovery Call"** (problem-led) and **"Book a Workshop" /
"Apply for the Bootcamp"** (education-led).

**No per-seat tiers, no self-serve checkout, no free trial, no Stripe
integration.**

Both Workshop and Pilot include qualifying criteria up front (size,
sector, system access, sponsor seniority) — the call-to-action is a
screen, not a lead capture.

## Lines We Don't Cross

These are hard editorial constraints that apply to every page, component,
and PR. They drive a **dedicated homepage section** ("Lines We Don't Cross")
that links to a **dedicated Trust & Governance page** for CISOs / DPOs /
Heads of Digital Transformation.

- **No employer surveillance framing.** No "see what your team is doing"
  copy, no manager dashboards, no per-employee activity timelines.
- **No productivity scoring.** No leaderboards, no per-worker output
  metrics, no "27% more productive" claims tied to individuals.
- **No sentiment monitoring.** No analysis of employee mood, tone, or
  wellbeing from communication content.
- **No manager-dashboard demos.** Demos depict a worker getting their own
  chores done — never a manager monitoring others.
- **No SaaS data extraction language.** No "the more you use Beings, the
  better it gets for everyone" cross-customer-training implications.
- **No SaaS pricing language.** No per-seat, no free trial, no self-serve.
- **Not a chatbot.** Beings is not "a better chatbot" and not "ChatGPT
  plugged into your files." Copy that conflates Beings with chatbot UX,
  even sympathetically, is rejected. Use the **mapping operations**
  framing instead.
- **No consumer "desktop app" framing.** The desktop binary is the
  technical delivery mechanism; the value prop is **locally deployed
  sovereign infrastructure**.
- **No legacy researcher / CloudLab / Aida / Interview Transcription
  positioning** — burn the boats. None of this survives into the new site.
- **No user-data demos.** All demos use synthetic data generated at build
  time. No real customer data, no anonymised customer corpora, no
  founder-inbox screenshots.

## Cloud Honesty Rule

The single most important claim on the site, and it must be made honestly.
Verbatim framing:

> Your memory, context, and data live locally and never leave your firewall.
> For complex reasoning, Beings orchestrates queries via zero-data-retention
> enterprise APIs (Claude / GPT-4) that legally cannot use your data for
> training. As your local hardware upgrades, those cloud workloads
> seamlessly shift to local models.

The site is **not allowed** to claim "all inference is local today." The
site **is required** to state that the data stays local while some reasoning
is delegated to **Zero-Retention Enterprise Cloud** endpoints, and that this
surface area is bounded and shrinking. This honesty constraint applies to
every page that touches AI / inference claims.

## Tech Stack

| Layer          | Technology                                                  |
|----------------|-------------------------------------------------------------|
| Framework      | Astro `^5.16` (`output: 'static'`, SSG)                     |
| Interactivity  | React `^19.2` islands (`client:visible`, `client:media`)    |
| Styling        | Tailwind CSS `^4.1` (via `@tailwindcss/vite`) + CSS custom properties |
| Motion         | `framer-motion ^12` (sparingly, only inside islands)        |
| Hosting        | Cloudflare Pages (`beings-marketing`)                       |
| Font           | Roboto Flex (Google Fonts)                                  |
| Content        | Astro pages, components, and MDX in git                     |

### Tech Stack Rationale

- **Static by default.** No database, no server runtime, no SSR. Edge-cached
  HTML serves in single-digit milliseconds.
- **Islands for interactivity.** React loads only on the components and routes
  that need JS. Almost everything renders as inert HTML.
- **Content as code.** Marketing copy lives in `.astro` and `.mdx` files in git.
  No CMS, no admin panel, no separate content database.
- **AI-first authoring.** The repo is laid out to be navigated and modified by
  Cursor/Composer-2. `AGENTS.md` and `llms.txt` are the entry points.
- **Brand DNA shared with product.** `src/styles/brand-tokens.css` mirrors the
  product design system so visual fidelity is automatic, not curated.

## Directory Structure

```
beings-website/
├── AGENTS.md                       # Agent identity (entry point)
├── llms.txt                        # This file (codebase routing map)
├── astro.config.mjs                # output: 'static', React + Tailwind integrations
├── package.json                    # Scripts: dev, build, preview, astro
├── wrangler.toml                   # Cloudflare Pages config (project: beings-marketing)
├── tsconfig.json
├── docs/
│   ├── architecture.md             # Narrative architecture
│   └── bw-prd-01-rebrand.xml       # PRD (SSOT)
├── planning/                       # Build-spec XML files (planning/NNNN-*.xml)
├── context/                        # Strategy and positioning docs
├── public/                         # Static assets (favicon, og-image, robots.txt)
├── src/
│   ├── assets/                     # Build-time assets (logo.svg, etc.)
│   ├── styles/
│   │   └── brand-tokens.css        # SSOT for colors, type, radii, elevation, motion
│   ├── components/
│   │   ├── app-shell/              # AppShell.astro, SidebarRail.astro, MainPanel.astro
│   │   ├── layout/                 # Layout.astro, Header.astro, Footer.astro, MobileMenu.tsx
│   │   ├── marketing/              # HeroCard, BentoCards, PricingTable, CTASection, etc.
│   │   ├── islands/                # React-only interactive components (.tsx)
│   │   ├── demos/                  # Product-look demos used inside marketing sections
│   │   └── ui/                     # Shared primitives
│   ├── content/                    # Astro content collections (when added; e.g. blog)
│   └── pages/                      # Route files: index, pricing, chat, privacy, terms, etc.
└── .cursor/
    ├── rules/                      # website-*.mdc + brand.mdc + core-* rules
    └── commands/
        └── prime.md                # /prime command (this command)
```

Note: layouts live under `src/components/layout/` and `src/components/app-shell/`
— there is intentionally no top-level `src/layouts/` directory. The PRD's
"layouts" terminology maps to these component folders.

## Brand Design System

### Source of Truth

`src/styles/brand-tokens.css` is the only place where colour, type, radius,
elevation, motion, and breakpoint values are defined. Components reference them
via CSS custom properties (`var(--color-primary)`, `var(--radius-md)`, etc.) or
through the Tailwind extension that aliases tokens to utility names.

**Never hardcode** a colour hex, radius value, shadow rule, or motion timing in
component code. If a token is missing, add it to `brand-tokens.css` first.

### Canvas + Card Philosophy

- **Canvas** — the page background, painted with `--color-background` (white in
  light mode, deep navy `#12121E` in dark mode). Fills the viewport.
- **Card** — white floating panels painted with `--color-surface`. Cards
  contain content and float above the canvas using the M3 elevation scale.
- **Depth** — apply elevation via `--elevation-1` through `--elevation-4`. Never
  use heavy custom drop-shadows.

### Radius Discipline

- `--radius-sm` = **8px** — buttons, inputs
- `--radius-md` = **12px** — cards, dialogs
- `--radius-lg` = **16px** — large cards, floating panels
- `--radius-xl` = **28px** — pills, composer-style inputs
- `--radius-full` — chips, toggles (stadium shape only)

Buttons are 8px radius, sentence case, semibold. **Never pill-shaped, never
uppercase.**

### Anti-Patterns

- No glassmorphism (`backdrop-filter: blur`).
- No heavy gradients.
- No over-rounding (do not round buttons past 8px).
- No heavy drop shadows (use the elevation scale).
- No card-in-card nesting on white surfaces.

### Dark Mode

Toggle by setting `data-theme="dark"` on `<html>`. All CSS custom properties
auto-switch via the `[data-theme="dark"]` selector in `brand-tokens.css`. No
component-level dark-mode conditionals are needed.

## Layout Systems

The site has **two coexisting layout systems**:

### 1. `AppShell` — Product-Feeling Pages

Lives in `src/components/app-shell/`. Three pieces:

- `AppShell.astro` — root CSS-grid layout (sidebar rail + main slot).
- `SidebarRail.astro` — 80px rail with product-style icons.
- `MainPanel.astro` — floating white panel inside the main slot.

Use `AppShell` for pages that should make the visitor feel like they are
*inside the product*: the homepage hero with shell context, an interactive
demo route, or anywhere "look like the product" is the brief.

### 2. `Layout` — Traditional Marketing Pages

Lives in `src/components/layout/`. Three pieces:

- `Layout.astro` — header + content + footer scaffold.
- `Header.astro` — full-width nav.
- `Footer.astro` — full-width footer with links and legal.
- `MobileMenu.tsx` — React island for the mobile hamburger menu.

Use `Layout` for traditional marketing pages: pricing, blog posts, legal
(`privacy.astro`, `terms.astro`), about, contact. Pick this layout when the
page does not need the product-shell framing.

## React Island Directives

React components live in `src/components/islands/` (and a few co-located in
`marketing/` and `layout/` for tight composition). Always specify the **narrowest
possible** client directive:

- `client:visible` — hydrates when the component scrolls into view. **Default
  choice for below-the-fold islands.** Examples: `ContactForm`, `CostCalculator`,
  `BentoCardsInteractive`.
- `client:media="(max-width: 767px)"` — hydrates only when a media query
  matches. Use for mobile-only widgets like `MobileMenu`.
- `client:idle` — hydrates when the browser is idle. Use for non-critical
  analytics or chat widgets.
- `client:load` — hydrates immediately on page load. **Use sparingly**, only
  for above-the-fold interactive elements that must respond before the user
  can scroll.
- `client:only="react"` — render only on the client (no SSR). Reserved for
  components that read browser-only APIs at first paint.

**Rule of thumb:** if the component does not need JavaScript, write it as
`.astro`, not `.tsx`.

## Content Authoring

- Marketing copy lives in `.astro` page files and (when added) MDX files in
  `src/content/`.
- Astro content collections (`src/content/config.ts`) provide typed schemas for
  long-form content like blog posts.
- **No CMS, no database, no admin UI.** All content is version-controlled in
  git and edited in the IDE with the rest of the codebase.
- **No PII flows through this workspace.** Marketing forms (HubSpot) POST to
  external endpoints; nothing is stored locally.

## Deployment Pipeline

```bash
npm install                          # Install dependencies
npm run dev                          # Local dev server (astro dev)
npm run build                        # Static build → dist/
npm run preview                      # Preview the built output locally
npx wrangler pages deploy dist       # Deploy to Cloudflare Pages
```

- Hosting: **Cloudflare Pages**, project `beings-marketing`.
- Custom domain: `https://website.beings.com`.
- Preview URL: `https://beings-marketing.pages.dev`.
- Branch deploys: configured at the Pages project level (auto-deploy on `main`
  is the intent; preview deploys per PR).

## Integration Points

All integrations are **client-side embeds** added to `Layout.astro` (or the
specific page that hosts them). No server-side proxying — the static site does
not have a backend.

- **HubSpot** (priority P0 per PRD) — tracking script + forms. Two form
  patterns: (a) HubSpot-hosted iframe embed; (b) custom React form island
  posting to HubSpot Forms API.
- **Analytics** (priority P0 per PRD) — **provider not yet set up.** The
  analytics tool has not been chosen and no tracking script is currently
  loaded by the site. Candidates under consideration include GTM and
  Plausible. Until a provider is selected, do not add tracking snippets to
  `Layout.astro`. See PRD `<integration name="Analytics">` for the open
  decision.
- **Cookie consent** (priority P0 per PRD) — CookieYes script tag in
  `Layout.astro`.
- **Cost calculator** (priority P1 per PRD) — `CostCalculator.tsx` island
  (planned; not yet implemented).

See `<integrations>` in `docs/bw-prd-01-rebrand.xml` for the canonical list.

## PRD-Driven Component Inventory (Summary)

The PRD (`docs/bw-prd-01-rebrand.xml`) maintains the canonical component
inventory with priority levels. Always consult the PRD before adding a new
component. High-level summary:

- **P0 (must have):** `AppShell`, `SidebarRail`, `MainPanel`, `Layout`,
  `Header`, `Footer`, `HeroMain`, `FeatureGrid`, `PricingTable`, `CTASection`,
  `ContactForm` (island).
- **P1 (should have):** `HeroSimple`, `FeatureShowcase`, `Testimonial`,
  `LogoCloud`, `BlogCard`, `BlogPost`, `CostCalculator` (island).
- **P2 (nice to have):** `TestimonialCarousel`, `StatsBar`, `ProductDemo`,
  `VideoPlayer`, `AuthorCard`.

## Performance Budget

- **Lighthouse 95+** on Performance, Accessibility, Best Practices, SEO for
  every public page (PRD goal G3).
- **SSG by default.** Pages render as inert HTML; no JS unless an island opts
  in.
- **Narrowest client directive wins.** Prefer `client:visible` over
  `client:load` whenever possible.
- **Inline critical CSS** via Astro's `inlineStylesheets: 'auto'` (already set
  in `astro.config.mjs`).
- **No client-side data fetching** for content that can be resolved at build
  time. Pass props from `.astro` frontmatter into islands.

## Accessibility Commitments

- Semantic HTML (`<header>`, `<nav>`, `<main>`, `<section>`, `<footer>`).
- ARIA labels on interactive controls that lack visible text.
- Keyboard navigation for every interactive surface (links, buttons, menu,
  dialogs).
- Visible focus rings using brand tokens (`focus:ring-2 focus:ring-primary-light`).
- Colour contrast meets WCAG AA against `--color-background` and
  `--color-surface` in both light and dark modes.
- No motion-only meaning. Animations honour `prefers-reduced-motion`.

## Out of Scope

- **No SSR.** The site is `output: 'static'`. Do not introduce server endpoints.
- **No CMS.** Content lives in git. Adding a headless CMS is a separate decision
  requiring PRD revision.
- **No database.** No D1, no Postgres, no KV.
- **No PII handling.** This is a GREEN workspace. Forms submit to external
  systems (HubSpot) and are not stored locally.
- **No authentication.** The site is fully public.
- **No `.dev.vars`.** Diskless dev — secrets (HubSpot keys, analytics IDs) come
  from Cloudflare Pages environment variables, never from a local file.

## Conventions and Anti-Patterns

- **Do not modify `brand-tokens.css`** without consulting the product workspace.
  These tokens are mirrored from the product design system.
- **Do not use React where Astro suffices.** If the component renders the same
  HTML on every visit, it is an `.astro` component.
- **Do not hardcode brand values.** Always go through `brand-tokens.css` and
  the Tailwind alias layer in `tailwind.config.mjs`.
- **Do not over-round.** Buttons stay at 8px, cards at 12px.
- **Do not introduce a backend.** If a feature needs a backend, surface the
  decision; do not silently add SSR or API routes.
